Operation 'CreateSender' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity on inputs (what fields are required), outputs (what is returned), and verification workflow. Enhanced version specifies this is for marketing campaigns, clarifies the verification process, and explains the 100-sender limit for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to create a new Sender.**

*You may create up to 100 unique Senders.*

Senders are required to be verified before use. If your domain has been authenticated, a new Sender will auto verify on creation. Otherwise an email will be sent to the `from.email`.
data.summary_suggestion: Create and verify a new marketing email sender
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new verified Sender (from address) for marketing campaigns. Accepts sender details including from email, name, and optional reply-to address. Returns the created Sender object with verification status. Auto-verifies if domain is authenticated; otherwise sends verification email to the from.email address. Limited to 100 unique Senders per account. Senders must be verified before use in campaigns.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity on inputs (what fields are required), outputs (what is returned), and verification workflow. Enhanced version specifies this is for marketing campaigns, clarifies the verification process, and explains the 100-sender limit for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Get a list of all Senders
data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to get a list of all your Senders.**
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all Senders associated with your account. Returns an array of Sender objects including verification status, email addresses, and metadata. Useful for discovering available senders before creating campaigns or checking sender verification status.
data.reasoning: Summary uses generic 'Get a list' phrasing. Description lacks information about pagination, return structure, and use cases. Enhanced version clarifies the response format and provides context for when an AI agent should use this operation.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to get the details for a specific Sender by `id`.**

data.reasoning: Summary is vague and doesn't specify the required ID parameter. Description lacks detail about what information is returned and practical use cases. Enhanced version clarifies the required input parameter and the complete response structure.

message: Operation 'UpdateSender' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is acceptable but description needs clarification on inputs (which fields can be updated), outputs (what is returned), and side effects (re-verification workflow). Enhanced version explicitly details the partial update behavior, re-verification process, and return value for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to update an existing Sender.**

Updates to `from.email` require re-verification. If your domain has been authenticated, a new Sender will auto verify on creation. Otherwise, an email will be sent to the `from.email`.

Partial updates are allowed, but fields that are marked as "required" in the `POST` (create) endpoint must not be nil if that field is included in the `PATCH` request.
data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing Sender's information using partial update semantics. Accepts optional fields including name, reply-to address, and from email. Changing the from.email address triggers re-verification: auto-verifies if domain is authenticated, otherwise sends verification email to the new address. Required fields (from POST endpoint) must not be null if included in the update. Returns the updated Sender object. Note: Partial updates are supported—only provided fields are modified.
data.reasoning: Summary is acceptable but description needs clarification on inputs (which fields can be updated), outputs (what is returned), and side effects (re-verification workflow). Enhanced version explicitly details the partial update behavior, re-verification process, and return value for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to delete an existing Sender.**
data.summary_suggestion: Delete a sender and remove from campaigns
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a Sender by its unique identifier. Requires the Sender ID as a path parameter. Returns a 204 No Content response on successful deletion. Deleted Senders cannot be used in new campaigns and will be removed from any scheduled campaigns. This action is irreversible.
data.reasoning: Summary is generic and doesn't convey the permanent nature of deletion. Description lacks critical details about the required ID parameter, response format, side effects (impact on campaigns), and irreversibility. Enhanced version clarifies these aspects for AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'ResetSenderVerification' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary doesn't clarify what is being verified or where the email is sent. Description lacks input requirements (Sender ID), output details (what is returned), and side effects (verification status remains pending). Enhanced version specifies the verification email destination, required parameter, response format, and verification workflow.

data.current_description: **This endpoint allows you to resend the verification request for a specific Sender.**
data.summary_suggestion: Resend verification email to sender address
data.description_suggestion: Resends the verification email to a Sender's from.email address. Requires the Sender ID as a path parameter. Use this when the initial verification email was not received or has expired. Returns the updated Sender object with verification status. The verification email will be sent to the from.email address associated with the Sender. Sender remains unverified until the verification link is clicked.
data.reasoning: Summary doesn't clarify what is being verified or where the email is sent. Description lacks input requirements (Sender ID), output details (what is returned), and side effects (verification status remains pending). Enhanced version specifies the verification email destination, required parameter, response format, and verification workflow.
